name: Unsecured Credentials in Files
description: Search common config locations for credential-shaped patterns (BEGIN PRIVATE KEY, password=, api_key=, AWS_SECRET, etc.) in the runner's own $HOME. MITRE ATT&CK T1552.001. Scope is limited to the runner's home directory, top-level dotfiles only (no recursion into project subtrees, no /etc, no other users' homes). Reports counts not contents — secret-shaped lines are NOT printed verbatim. Read-only. Safety: refuse-by-default unless --target <whitelisted_IP> or --unsafe-local.
exec: echo '--- credential-shaped pattern scan (counts only, contents redacted) ---'; for pat in 'BEGIN PRIVATE KEY' 'BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE' 'password=' 'passwd=' 'api_key=' 'aws_secret' 'AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY' 'token='; do n=$(grep -c -i "$pat" "$HOME"/.[a-zA-Z]* 2>/dev/null | awk -F: '{s+=$2} END {print s+0}'); echo "pattern='$pat' matches=$n"; done
